name: Monthly Check-in: Alignment Analysis
description: Maps the officer's activities against their performance agreement goals, team priorities, and development objectives. Surfaces gaps and unaligned work.
prompt_template:
<task>
Now I want to assess how my activities from {{month_period}} align to my goals. Below are my current performance agreement goals, team priorities, and development objectives.

For each of my performance goals, identify:
1. ACTIVITIES THAT CONTRIBUTED — which items from my activity inventory directly supported this goal
2. PROGRESS ASSESSMENT — based on what I did this month, is this goal on track / needs attention / at risk? (Note: this is your assessment based only on what I've told you — I'll verify)
3. GAPS — are there goals where I did little or no work this month? Flag them honestly.

Then do the same for team priorities (if provided) and development goals (if provided).

Finally, flag any UNALIGNED WORK — activities that consumed significant time but don't map to any stated goal. This isn't necessarily bad (reactive work, emerging priorities, BAU) but it should be visible.

Present as a structured alignment map, not a wall of text.
</task>

<performance_goals>
{{performance_goals}}
</performance_goals>

<team_goals>
{{team_goals}}
</team_goals>

<development_goals>
{{development_goals}}
</development_goals>

<activity_inventory>
[Paste or reference the output from Step 1]
</activity_inventory>

<framework_context>
The APSC non-SES Performance Framework assesses both outcomes (what was delivered) and behaviours (how it was delivered). APS Values to consider: Impartial, Committed to service, Accountable, Respectful, Ethical. {{agency_performance_framework}}
</framework_context>

variables.name: performance_goals
variables.description: The officer's current performance agreement goals or KPIs

variables.name: team_goals
variables.description: Team or branch priorities for the period

variables.name: development_goals
variables.description: Current development plan or professional goals

variables.name: agency_performance_framework
variables.description: Agency-specific performance framework elements beyond the APSC default

beginner.notes: Pay particular attention to goals flagged as 'needs attention' or 'at risk'. Do you agree with the assessment? The AI can only judge based on what you told it — your knowledge of the full picture matters.

intermediate.notes: Review the 'unaligned work' section carefully. If significant time went to things not in your goals, consider whether this reflects an emerging priority that should be discussed with your supervisor.

advanced.notes: Consider running this step twice — once against your personal goals, once against your team's business plan or branch strategy. Feed both into Step 5 for a richer alignment picture.

known_limitations: Alignment assessments are based only on what you've told the AI. Verify the assessment against your own knowledge — the AI doesn't know about context you haven't shared.
Progress ratings (on track / needs attention / at risk) are the AI's inference from limited information. Treat them as prompts for reflection, not judgements.
